To create a client, pass your block library and access token to the
API requests must be authenticated by providing an API access token. Learn more in the
which may emit up to two values for a given request:
The cached version of the story if one is available locally.
The latest version from the API, unless it matches the cached version already emitted.
The decoded content type is inferred from the value you bind. Most often that is the block library itself, giving you a
You can also consume the publisher with

code: let story: Story<Content>? = try await client.story("articles/hello-world")

inlineContent.text: into a polymorphic decoder keyed on the Storyblok
Each case corresponds to a component. By default the case name must match the component’s technical name in Storyblok:

code: struct Hero: Decodable { let title: String }
struct Article: Decodable { let headline: String }

content.text: Nested struct content vs. labeled fields

inlineContent.text: A case may carry its fields in one of two ways:
, declared inside the enum, which decodes the entire content object. Every nested struct must have a matching case.
, which decode individual fields of the content object directly.

code: // Whole content object decoded into a nested struct
// Individual fields decoded from the content object
case header(altTitle: String, altSubtitle: String, altImage: Field.Asset)

inlineContent.text: When a Swift identifier can’t match the API name (for example a technical name containing a dash, or a field whose JSON key differs), provide a mapping enum:
to act as a catch-all. Components not matched by any other case decode as
instead of throwing, so your app handles new or unrecognised components gracefully:

code: case unknown   // any other component decodes here
struct Page: Decodable { let title: String }

inlineContent.text: directly — useful when a story always contains a single component. Implement
only if the type has relations to resolve (it defaults to an empty string):
type provides two initializers. The convenience initializer
lets you supply your own preconfigured session.
Configuring default parameters for all requests is as simple as passing them to the convenience initializer:
These parameters map directly to the ones accepted by
For more control, create the underlying

code: let client = StoryblokClient(library: Content.self, session: session)

inlineContent.text: When you’re done using the client, call
to release the resources held by the underlying session:
Stories can be fetched by slug or UUID:
is inferred from how you bind the result. Binding to the block library lets you switch over every component:

code: let story: Story<Content>? = try await client.story("home").values.first { _ in true }

inlineContent.text: When you know a story always contains one specific component, you can bind directly to a nested block struct (or any
type matching the content shape) to skip the

code: let story: Story<Content.Page>? = try await client.story("home").values.first { _ in true }

Replace the referenced UUID string in the response with the matching object from the
Decode the resolved relation as a nested

code: case popular(articles: [Story<Content>])    // lists are also supported
let author: Story<Content>               // nested-struct relations too

inlineContent.text: must be the enclosing block library
or one of its nested structs, so the macro can discover its relation fields.
controls how deeply relations are followed:
Higher values resolve relations of relations.
fields fail decoding — model relation fields as
to receive the raw UUIDs instead.
When relations form a cycle, resolution stops at the configured depth. How the boundary is handled depends on whether the field is optional:
at the cycle boundary, breaking the cycle gracefully.
Model fields that may participate in cycles as optional, or as a plain
The maximum number of relations that can be resolved is 50 stories per request. This is a Storyblok API limitation.
Rich text content from Storyblok is decoded into the structured
is generic over your block library so that embedded component blocks decode into the same types as the rest of your content. Declare a
property on a block to decode rich text in a type-safe manner:
enum, covering bold, italic, underline, strike, code, subscript, superscript, links (
Composite rich text nodes conform to the
function to recursively yield all nested leaf nodes:

when its type varies, or as the concrete
. When fetching stories the client emits values using a
The client first attempts to retrieve the story from cache (using
It then makes a network request to get the latest version, retrying transient failures.
Duplicates are filtered so the cached and fresh responses are only emitted once when identical.
API errors and decoding failures are wrapped in
, the same error is thrown from the
sequence and can be caught with
